description_long: This object consists of several tall, slender utility poles arranged on a flat, concrete-like base. The poles are metallic and feature multiple crossarms at varying heights, with some crossarms supporting a series of insulators and attached wires. One pole has a distinctive multi-armed top, resembling a spider or starburst, likely for supporting multiple power lines. The wires are thin and extend horizontally between the poles, simulating a network of overhead electrical lines. The overall structure is utilitarian, with a weathered, industrial appearance typical of urban or suburban power distribution infrastructure.
description: A group of metallic utility poles with crossarms and attached wires, arranged on a concrete base and featuring a multi-armed top for power distribution.
description_view_0: From this view, the multi-armed top of the central pole and the arrangement of crossarms and wires are clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the alignment of the poles and the horizontal extension of the wires, with the multi-armed pole prominent in the center.
description_view_2: Seen from this side, the staggered heights of the poles and the network of wires are more apparent, with the crossarms visible in profile.
description_view_3: This perspective shows the back of the arrangement, with the multi-armed pole to the left and the other poles and wires receding into the background.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: There are multiple crossarms, mostly horizontal, with one pole featuring a multi-armed top and others having single or double crossarms at different heights.
